1.description: Retrieves supporting knowledge content from the knowledge corpus — articles, runbooks, playbooks, SOPs, troubleshooting guides, known-issue write-ups, previous resolutions, and reference material. Two modes: TOPIC search (no entity — e.g. 'how do I fix VPN') and ENTITY-SCOPED search (KB articles ABOUT a record / CI / asset). Renders article title + body (Symptom / Cause / Resolution as present) + source citation. Use this agent when the user wants MATERIAL THAT HELPS UNDERSTAND, RESOLVE, OR SUPPORT a topic or record — not the record's own stored field values. Capability is bounded to the KB corpus; this agent does NOT render the record's own fields (status, priority, owner, SLA, root_cause, RCA, linked-record ids, or any other on-record attribute).

skills.name: Search the knowledge base
skills.description: Retrieve ranked knowledge content - articles, runbooks, SOPs, troubleshooting guides, how-tos, known-issue write-ups - that helps understand or resolve a topic or issue. Two modes: topic search (no record) and entity-scoped (KB about a record). Use when the user wants supporting material OUTSIDE the record. NOT for the record's own field values, finding similar tickets, or modifying records.
use_when: the user asks how to fix, how to do, or how something was solved
the user wants documentation, a KB article, runbook, SOP, procedure, or guidance for a topic
a general IT how-to question with no specific record (e.g. how to reset MFA, VPN error steps, mapping a network drive)
an everyday end-user workplace-IT task even if it sounds mundane - browser, email client, Wi-Fi, printer, laptop/device setup (e.g. clear browser cache, set up Outlook)
the user asks what is documented or what guidance exists for an issue or a record
the user wants troubleshooting steps or a known-issue / known-error write-up
not_when: the user wants facts, fields, status, or timeline of a specific record itself (route to uc01_summarization)
the user wants the value of an on-record field such as root_cause or affected_ci (that is a field-read, route to uc01_summarization)
the user wants other tickets similar to or duplicates of a record (route to uc02_similar_tickets)
the user wants to create, update, or modify a record (route to an action/fulfilment agent)
the user wants to triage, prioritise, or route a ticket (route to uc05_triage)
examples: what's the runbook for a stuck job
documentation on mapping a network drive
how do I configure Outlook on a new laptop
is there a guide for SSO login issues
